summary.intent: Update the homepage description sentence in the agents-md-evaluator app to accurately reflect all supported AI coding agents (Claude, GitHub Copilot, Cursor) and all types of artifacts detected (context files, rules, skills, linked docs).
summary.outcome: Updated EmptyState.tsx with a new hero tagline highlighting Claude, GitHub Copilot, and Cursor by name, added a gradient CSS class for visual emphasis, and committed the changes with all pre-commit checks passing (1329 tests, lint, TypeScript).

repo: Project is agents-md-evaluator, supporting three AI coding agents: Claude (CLAUDE.md, .claude/rules/, .claude/skills/), GitHub Copilot (copilot-instructions.md, .github/instructions/), and Cursor (.cursor/rules/, .cursor/skills/).
Artifact types span four categories: context files, rules files (.md/.mdc), skill files (SKILL.md variants), and referenced/linked documentation.
Pre-commit hooks run 1329 tests plus linting and TypeScript checks automatically before every commit.
Homepage hero copy lives in frontend/src/components/EmptyState.tsx; visual styling in frontend/src/styles.css.

code.finding: Hero tagline uses inline spans for emphasis; a new hero-tagline-emphasis class was applied to highlight agent names with gradient styling.
Added .hero-tagline-emphasis class using a blue-to-purple gradient text effect; follows existing gradient text conventions in the design system.

workflow: When multiple Edit attempts fail on a file, falling back to Write (full file overwrite) is an effective escape hatch.
Verifying CSS class names via Grep before editing (hero-tagline|hero-headline) avoids naming collisions with existing styles.

friction: EmptyState.tsx required 4 Edit attempts plus a final Write call to land the correct content, indicating repeated partial failures with the Edit tool on this file.
open_items: The file-list description in the tagline was generalized ('rules files, skills, and linked documentation') rather than exhaustively enumerated — may want to revisit if the supported file set changes significantly.
No explicit test coverage added for the updated copy strings; snapshot or content tests could catch future regressions.
